title: Wish and if only
explanation:
Wish and if only

Use wish / if only + past simple to talk about present regrets, wish / if only + past perfect for past regrets, and wish + would to complain about annoying behaviour.

questions.text: I wish I [*had|have|would have] more free time these days.
questions.feedback: Present regret uses past simple form.

questions.text: If only we [*had booked|booked|would book] the tickets earlier.
She wishes her neighbour [*would stop|stopped|had stopped] playing loud music at night.
I wish I [*knew|know|had known] the answer right now.
If only they [*had not sold|did not sell|would not sell] the old house.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: He wishes he ____ speak more confidently in meetings. | *could | can | had
If only I ____ more carefully before sending the email. | *had checked | checked | would check
I wish it ____ raining every weekend. | *would stop | stopped | had stopped
She wishes she ____ closer to her parents now. | *lived | had lived | would live
If only I [*had remembered|remembered|would remember] his birthday.
We wish the bus [*would arrive|arrived|had arrived] on time for once.
He wishes he [*had not said|did not say|would not say] that in the meeting.
If only they [*were|had been|are] more reliable.
questions.feedback: Present regret about a current situation.
